goal: Audit the full path from pocket-ligand inputs to backend-ready candidates so metric degradation can be attributed to concrete pipeline mechanisms, not only final Vina/GNINA numbers.

degradation_mechanisms: ligand and pocket coordinates can be evaluated in inconsistent frames
pocket radius or centroid can be inferred from a protein crop rather than the intended binding pocket
candidate export can double-apply or omit coordinate_frame_origin
pipeline_stages.failure_signal: large docking degradation with little internal ligand geometry change

degradation_mechanisms: slot collapse can remove local pocket information before generation
pooled summaries can hide atom-level pocket contacts
off-modality leakage can blur topology, geometry, and context roles
pipeline_stages.failure_signal: raw_flow candidates have acceptable global pocket proxies but poor backend affinity

degradation_mechanisms: weak geo_from_pocket gates can under-condition coordinates on pocket geometry
overly dense gates can collapse controlled interaction into noisy fusion
gate summaries alone cannot diagnose which pocket tokens influence which atoms
pipeline_stages.failure_signal: candidate placement is globally plausible but locally incompatible with receptor atoms

degradation_mechanisms: current baseline velocity head mostly uses pooled conditioning summaries
per-atom velocity can miss local ligand-ligand geometry and receptor clashes
translation invariance is tested, but rotation consistency and local pocket equivariance are not yet sufficient
pipeline_stages.failure_signal: raw_flow Vina/GNINA is poor before any repair or reranking

degradation_mechanisms: hard constraints can move candidates away from a dockable raw pose
atom-type sampling can generate chemically difficult candidates even if coordinates are plausible
coordinate noise or clipping can introduce receptor clashes
pipeline_stages.failure_signal: constrained_flow is worse than raw_flow on docking backends

degradation_mechanisms: distance-only bond inference can create bad valence or synthetic accessibility patterns
chemistry refinement can change the backend molecular payload even when coordinates are fixed
coordinate-moving repair can be incorrectly mixed with bond-only refinement
pipeline_stages.failure_signal: SA/QED or RDKit validity degrades while coordinate-level docking stays similar

degradation_mechanisms: centroid anchoring can translate candidates away from their raw dockable pose
pocket declash can push atoms into worse docking-box geometry
full repair combines coordinate movement and bond inference, making attribution difficult
pipeline_stages.failure_signal: centroid_only or full_repair produces large positive Vina/GNINA deltas

degradation_mechanisms: weak pocket-fit proxies can select candidates that dock badly
backend export can silently change atoms, coordinates, or docking boxes
posthoc backend-aware ranking can be mistaken for native model quality
pipeline_stages.failure_signal: reranked or exported candidates improve proxy metrics but not backend docking

diagnostic_order: First verify coordinate frame and backend export, because mistakes here can dominate all downstream metrics.
Then compare raw_flow versus constrained_flow before repair, because native model quality must be isolated.
Then separate coordinate-preserving chemistry refinement from coordinate-moving repair.
Only after these checks should proxy reranking or preference data be used.
claim_boundary: Only raw_flow or explicitly model-native coordinate-preserving outputs may support model improvement claims. Repaired, reranked, or backend-aware posthoc outputs remain diagnostic unless separately justified.
